DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ISSUE, EVIDENTIARY, AND MONETARY SANCTIONS SUBMITTED ON SEP-28-2023 BY PETER CATALANOTTI. APPEARANCE BY COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFFS. NO APPEARANCES BY DEFENDANTS. PARTY STIPULATES TO JUDGE PRO TEM DECIDING THE MATTER. DISCUSSION HELD AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. THE COURT REQUESTS THAT COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT IN WORD FORMAT, LEAVING IN THE PARAGRAPH WITH THE MONETARY SANCTIONS, AND THE COURT WILL TAKE ANOTHER LOOK AT IT. SINCE THE DEFENDANTS DO NOT HAVE AN EMAIL ADDRESS ON FILE, THE COURT REQUESTS THAT THE PLAINTIFF EMAIL THE JUDGE PRO TEM CATALANOTTI THE ORDER AND THEN ONCE IT IS SIGNED AND FILED THE PLAINTIFF WILL MAIL A COPY TO THE LAST KNOWN ADDRESS OF THE DEFENDANTS. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI; CLERK: R. SAHATJIAN. NOT REPORTED. (DISCOVERY 302).

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS CARRIE LIN, YUTONG ZHANG'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS JUNYI CAI ALFONSO AND NELS ALFONSO, INC.. MOTION GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANTS' OPERATIVE ANSWER TO PLAINTIFFS' OPERATIVE COMPLAINT SHALL BE STRICKEN. DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS. THE COURT LASTLY REMINDS THE PARTIES TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULES GOVERNING DISCOVERY MOTIONS. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $8,000.00 ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF TO BE PAID JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY BY DEFENDANTS. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM WILLIAM LYNN AT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Junyi Cai Alfonso And Nels Alfonso, Inc.
Off Calendar Dec-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
JULIA L. COVELLO, ESQ., BERLINER COHEN, LLP (408) 286-5800 julia.covello@berliner.com ATTORNEYS FOR PLAINTIFFS CARRIE FAN LIN AND YUTONG ZHANG for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Email recieved by plaintiff's counsel stating that they did not include defendants in the email to the court informing them that plaintiff's counsel would be appearing for today's hearing. Plaintiffs counsel does not have any current email addresses for defendants and that all motions and communications have been by US mail.

Plaintff's counsel stipulates to Judge Pro Tem Willim Lynn.

Plaintiff seeks clarification as to monetary sanctions. Matter discussed, not ARGUED.

The Court modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS CARRIE LIN, YUTONG ZHANG'S Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Junyi Cai Alfonso And Nels Alfonso, Inc.

Motion granted, no opposition filed. "[S]anctions are generally imposed in an incremental approach, with terminating sanctions being the last resort." Dept. of Forestry & Fire Protection v. Howell, 18 Cal. App. 5th 154, 191 (2017). Where a violation is willful, preceded by a history of abuse, and the evidence shows that less severe sanctions would not produce compliance with the discovery rules, the trial court is justified in imposing the ultimate sanction. August B. Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc., 174 Cal. App. 4th 967, 992 (2009). It is abundantly clear in this case that the Defendants have chosen to willfully disobey court orders by not appearing at court hearings and complying with discovery orders, the ultimate sanction is therefore justified. Defendants' operative answer to Plaintiffs' operative complaint shall be stricken. Default and default judgment shall be entered against all Defendants. The Court lastly reminds the parties to comply with local rules governing discovery motions. Sanctions in the amount of $8,000.00 are awarded to plaintiff to be paid jointly and severally by defendants.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
